reasoning: The Lean statement unfolds exactly the definition of "P.subgroupOf H is a Sylow p-subgroup of H". In Mathlib, Sylow p G is a structure with two fields: isPGroup' (IsPGroup p toSubgroup) and is_maximal' (maximality among p-subgroups). The Lean conclusion is a conjunction of these two properties applied to P.subgroupOf H inside H, which is the standard encoding of "P ∈ Syl_p(H)" when P ≤ H. The hypothesis hH : P ≤ H captures "H contains P" (the coercion from Sylow to Subgroup makes this well-typed). No extra hypotheses are introduced and the quantifier structure matches the Mathlib Sylow definition exactly.
